specific_mechanism: 12 main meridians + 8 extraordinary meridians connect specific internal organ systems to specific surface acupuncture points (~360 named points). Treatment proceeds by stimulating an acupoint to modulate qi flow along its meridian, producing therapeutic effect at the connected organ. The intervention is local (a single point) but the effect is pathway-mediated (along the meridian) and distal (at the connected organ).
llm_application: Map LLM agent internal capabilities to 'meridians' (defined pathways through residual stream / attention layers) connecting specific 'intervention sites' (token positions, attention heads) to specific 'downstream capabilities' (output behaviors). Build a cartography of (intervention site → downstream effect) pairs as a deployment-time artifact. Targeted interventions at intervention sites modulate downstream capabilities along defined pathways without disturbing other capabilities.
novelty_claim: LLM activation steering is heavily published: Steer Like the LLM (2605.03907) learns token-specific steering coefficients; Locate, Steer, and Improve (2601.14004) framework directly maps intervention sites; Local Linearity (2604.19018) uses control theory for steering; Guiding Giants (2505.20309) Weighted Activation Steering; Activation Steering with Dynamic Rejection (2603.06745). AI+acupuncture exists for actual acupoint localization (Springer s13020-025-01173-3); reverse direction. The TCM-meridian-as-LLM-pathway analogy isn't directly imported but activation steering covers the mechanism.
